ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Filing Accounting Or Other Dispositive Petition On Death Of Personal Representative
Off Calendar Dec-02-2020 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Appointment Of Successor Personal Representative
Off Calendar Oct-07-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Amy Shelf, Esq., counsel for Kurt Thompson, Administrator (deceased)(appearing by video conference).

Court and counsel note the difficulties occasioned by the death of the Administrator, Mr. Thompson. The Court refers Ms. Shelf to Probate Code Sec. 10953(c) which states that legal counsel for the personal representative is required to file an accounting when the personal representative is no longer able to serve and no successor personal representative is in place. Ms. Shelf advises the Court that pursuant to an Order of the San Mateo Superior Court, the principal, if not sole, asset of the decedent - certain real estate - is a trust asset and not an estate asset. As such, the estate may be devoid of assets. The Court notes the provisions of Probate Code Sec. 12251 and that Ms. Shelf may want to consider those provisions when filing the accounting petition.

The Court sets hearing on the status of filing the accounting petition - or other dispositive petition - for December 2, 2020 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204. If the relevant petitions are filed prior to that date, no appearance is required.

Judge: Hon. Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Reporter: Not Reported; Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (D204)
